rationale: Both miss dsocek's specific packed_modules_mapping/naming-conflict point (likely tied to a since-removed part of the PR not visible in the truncated diff), but X's suggestion to derive default_components from pipeline._dit_modules echoes the same 'stop hardcoding, use existing single source of truth' spirit, giving it partial credit Y lacks entirely (Y's sweep even concludes the hardcoded approach is 'the right design'). X's findings are all tightly grounded in the actual changed code (component-scan safety, encoder LoRA silent no-op) and highly actionable; Y's two nits about unrelated pipelines' stale unet docstrings are honestly-flagged as pre-existing/out-of-scope, diluting precision even though its verification sweep methodology was thorough.
